topic: New topic: inference for nonparametric partial-identification bound intersections over rich or continuous covariate level sets. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Manski nonparametric bounds, Imbens-Manski confidence intervals, Chernozhukov-Hong-Tamer set inference, and Andrews-Shi moment-inequality inference. Our theorem is not that because the object is the nonregular endpoint functional formed by intersecting estimated level-set bounds as the active level-set geometry changes. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a tangent-cone or EIF-style phase threshold separating unique-active, finite-multi-active, and continuum-active flat-face regimes, plus a hand-derived bootstrap-failure or limit-law witness. If the proposal reduces to ordinary interval intersection, generic moment inequalities, or finite-cell Manski bounds, pivot or stop early.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop a tangent-cone or EIF-style phase calculus for nonparametric partial-identification endpoints formed by intersecting covariate-level bounds over rich or continuous level sets.

source_refs.claim: Intersection bounds are sup/inf functionals over potentially infinite inequalities and require active inequality selection for inference.
source_refs.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam M. Rosen

source_refs.claim: For directionally differentiable maps, ordinary bootstrap consistency requires full differentiability when the first-stage limit is Gaussian.
source_refs.author: Zheng Fang, Andres Santos

source_refs.excerpt: continuous covariates would require an additional measurability and regularization layer
outside that finite set there is a unique active pair

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published intersection-bound and directionally-differentiable inference anchors cover sup/inf and nonsmooth maps, while the prior RDD proposal explicitly excludes continuous covariates; none of these sources supplies a phase diagram for the same endpoint when the active covariate level set moves among a singleton, finite ties, and a continuum flat face.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to apply intersection-bound inference or a single EIF verbatim fails because the active covariate contact set can change with the local law, and Fang-Santos bootstrap theory flags exactly this lack of full differentiability at ties and flat faces.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A statement-level theorem giving the directional derivative or tangent-cone support functional for the endpoint, with separate limiting laws for unique-active, finite-multi-active, and continuum-active regimes.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a hand-derived bootstrap-failure witness for naive plug-in, percentile, or centered multiplier bootstrap inference on an estimated bound-intersection endpoint.

source_refs.claim: The standard bootstrap can fail for directionally but not fully differentiable functionals even though a directional delta method still gives a limit law.
source_refs.author: Zheng Fang, Andres Santos

source_refs.claim: Scalar interval inference is nonuniform when the identified interval width approaches zero, so ordinary endpoint normal approximations do not settle local boundary cases.
source_refs.author: Guido W. Imbens, Charles F. Manski

source_refs.excerpt: bootstrap band calibrated to the centered oracle quantile has coverage loss
while the coordinatewise drift is o(1)

open_problems.why_unsolved: The general bootstrap-failure theorem is known, and interval-width nonuniformity is known, but the literature and prior proposals do not provide a compact partial-identification witness showing how an estimated level-set contact gap creates random bootstrap drift for an intersection endpoint.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to bootstrap the finite or continuum max/min of estimated bounds fails because the bootstrap conditions on the estimated active gap, while Fang-Santos and the prior undercoverage witness show that centered resampling can miss the non-Gaussian directional derivative when coordinatewise drift accumulates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A two-level or continuum-contact example computing both the population limit law and the conditional bootstrap law, proving nonconvergence or undercoverage for the naive bootstrap.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct adaptive inference for continuum-active contact sets in nonparametric covariate-level bound intersections rather than relying on conservative generic conditional-moment-inequality sets.

source_refs.claim: Conditional moment inequality inference gives uniformly valid confidence sets using generalized moment selection.
source_refs.author: Donald W. K. Andrews, Xiaoxia Shi

source_refs.claim: Weighted KS inference for conditional moment inequalities changes rates and asymptotic behavior, requiring new critical-value theory.
source_refs.author: Timothy B. Armstrong

source_refs.claim: Functional inequality testing can use nonparametric bootstrap with estimated contact sets.
source_refs.author: Sokbae Lee, Kyungchul Song, Yoon-Jae Whang

source_refs.excerpt: whether the essential-envelope sharpness argument for arbitrary Borel supports needs a measurable-selection lemma
simultaneous valid bands for the two observable fiber-extrema functionals

open_problems.why_unsolved: CMI and functional-inequality procedures control broad inequality systems, while the prior Borel-support proposal isolates measurable-selection and simultaneous-band obstacles for rich-support extrema; the missing object is endpoint-specific inference that adapts to the size and geometry of the contact set.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to use an off-the-shelf CMI or functional-inequality band fails because Armstrong documents rate changes for conditional moment inequalities and Lee-Song-Whang contact-set methods target tests, not a sharp endpoint law over an estimated Borel contact set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A confidence procedure with an estimated enlarged contact set, bias or undersmoothing conditions for the bound maps, and a uniform coverage theorem across singleton, finite-tie, and continuum-contact sequences.

open_problems.open_problem: Bridge finite-dimensional flat-face inference for partially identified moment-inequality models with infinite-dimensional covariate-contact endpoints generated by bound intersections.

source_refs.claim: Random-set and support-function methods give asymptotic theory for partially identified sets whose support functions are finite-dimensional objects.
source_refs.author: Arie Beresteanu, Francesca Molinari

source_refs.claim: Calibrated projection builds confidence intervals for components and smooth functions of partially identified parameters in moment-inequality models.
source_refs.author: Hiroaki Kaido, Francesca Molinari, Joerg Stoye

source_refs.claim: General partial-identification set inference focuses on moment inequalities and equalities, with robustness under local perturbations treated at the set level.
source_refs.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Han Hong, Elie Tamer

source_refs.excerpt: with kinks when the binding constraint switches between the McCrary envelope and one or more covariate-share caps
The tangent phase is the set of local t-values

open_problems.why_unsolved: Finite-dimensional support functions, projections, and constraint qualifications describe faces of identified sets or moment-inequality regions, while the CausalSmith prior proposals point to endpoint kinks and tangent phases; the unresolved bridge is when the continuum covariate contact set reduces to a finite face and when it remains an infinite-dimensional Gaussian-supremum problem.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to import finite-dimensional projection or support-function inference fails because the active face in the bound-intersection problem is a covariate contact set with smoothing bias and topology, not only a face of a finite moment-inequality polyhedron.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A local optimization theorem whose limiting program is indexed by the active covariate contact set, including sufficient conditions for finite-dimensional reduction and counterconditions yielding an infinite-dimensional support-functional limit.

literature_map:
Manski (1990, American Economic Review) supplies the baseline nonparametric treatment-effect bounds that motivate covariate-level bound maps, but not endpoint inference over moving level sets.
Manski and Pepper (2000, Econometrica) show how monotone IV restrictions sharpen bounds through covariate-indexed inequalities.
Imbens and Manski (2004, Econometrica) solve scalar partially identified interval inference and identify nonuniformity as interval width shrinks.
Chernozhukov, Hong, and Tamer (2007, Econometrica) give general confidence regions for parameter sets in moment inequality/equality models.
Beresteanu and Molinari (2008, Econometrica) give random-set and support-function asymptotics for partially identified sets.
Andrews and Shi (2013, Econometrica; 2014, Journal of Econometrics) develop conditional moment inequality inference and generalized moment selection.
Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen (2013, Econometrica) is the closest direct anchor: intersection bounds as sup/inf over parametric or nonparametric functions with active inequality selection.
Armstrong (2014, Journal of Econometrics) shows conditional moment inequality KS statistics have rate and critical-value changes under inverse-variance weighting.
Lee, Song, and Whang (2018, Econometric Theory) use estimated contact sets for nonparametric functional inequality tests.
Kaido, Molinari, and Stoye (2019, Econometrica) handle calibrated projection inference for finite-dimensional partially identified parameters.
Fang and Santos (2019, Review of Economic Studies) supply the needed nonsmooth delta-method and bootstrap-failure anchor for directionally differentiable functionals.
The remaining gap is not ordinary interval intersection or generic CMI inference: it is endpoint inference for level-set bound intersections whose active geometry changes among singleton, finite tie, and continuum flat-face regimes.
prior_proposal_map:
Active research/pid_bound_intersection_inference has no completed proposal artifact yet; Track A found only adjacent banked proposals.
pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 is the closest PartialID substrate: finite covariate-cell cap/envelope endpoints, active-budget kinks, and an explicit continuous-covariate scope flag.
pid_rdd_flow_manipulation_v1 angle0_rejected supplies many-neighborhood intersection-bound and support-function-oracle warnings.
pid_text_treatment_distortion_v1 supplies rich-support fiber-extrema, simultaneous-band language, and an open Borel measurable-selection concern.
pid_dose_response_shape_v1 supplies a pasting gap: pointwise sharp continuous-dose bounds may not imply one joint sharp curve, but that was kept out of emitted problems as adjacent rather than central.
panel_honest_did_phase_v1 angle1_rejected supplies active-face/tie/tangent-phase syntax useful as a template, but it is panel rather than PartialID covariate-bound theory.
eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 angle3_rejected supplies a hand-derived centered-bootstrap undercoverage witness pattern.
No prior proposal already states continuum-active flat-face inference for nonparametric partial-ID bound intersections.
